entries.summary: Phase 3 Data Layer Audit complete. Audited vector_store.py (644 lines), document_processor.py (262 lines), reranking.py (87 lines), query_transformer.py (53 lines), utils.py (23 lines), and seed_loader.py (149 lines). Produced 39 total findings (3 CRITICAL, 9 HIGH, 15 MEDIUM, 12 LOW) across 3 evidence files. Key discoveries: BM25 index management broken across multiple files, entire seed_loader module is dead code (never instantiated), race conditions in vector_store concurrent access, pervasive happy-path-only error handling. Architect caught 2 false positives from explorer (python-pptx API misunderstanding, severity overestimate on internal data).

metadata.plan_id: Comprehensive Codebase QA Review — AI-Hardened Edition

top_rejection_reasons: false_positive: explorer incorrectly flagged python-pptx shape.has_table/table as CRITICAL
severity_downgrade: seed_loader KeyError on internal data rated CRITICAL but should be HIGH
lessons_learned: Explorer may misattribute CRITICAL severity to internal-data issues — architect must validate severity calibration for non-user-facing data paths
python-pptx API specifics (has_table/table relationship) require domain knowledge to validate — explorer may flag false positives on library-specific patterns
Dead code detection via grep is highly reliable — SeedDataLoader having zero callers was confirmed immediately
BM25 index management is a systemic issue spanning vector_store and seed_loader — cross-boundary verification in Phase 8 should validate this pattern across all data paths
Silent exception swallowing (happy-path-only error handling) is the most common AI smell in the data layer — appears in document_processor, seed_loader, and vector_store
